competitor_patterns: Bikerumor dominates with 24,868-word mega-guides that test 15+ products, include comparison tables, FAQ schema, and are updated with explicit editor timestamps (e.g. 'Updated May 21, 2026') — recency signals are baked into H1 and snippet
Top-ranking pages (Bikerumor, Cyclist) lead with trust signals in meta descriptions: 'We tested 18 of the best' and 'expert reviewer picks' — quantified testing authority drives CTR from position 5-8
Cyclist.co.uk uses a dedicated 'How do we test and why trust us?' H2 section to justify recommendations — editorial credibility section is a structural requirement in top-3 content
All top-3 pages include both FAQ schema and comparison tables — no exceptions; pages without both elements do not rank in top 3 for 'best cycling glasses 2026' queries

Competitor snippets consistently surface specific lens technology language ('changes highlights and contrasts of certain light wavelengths') and fit details ('arms into helmet vents') — technical specificity in the opening 160 chars wins featured snippets
our_gaps: Word count gap is critical: Bikerumor ranks #1 with 24,868 words; our best-performing pages cap at 8,148 words and most cluster around 3,000-4,000 — we have no mega-guide that matches top-competitor depth for the 264-impression 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' query
33% FAQ schema coverage means 27 out of 41 pages have no FAQ schema — this directly suppresses our appearance in Google AI Overviews and rich results for near-top keywords averaging position 6-8

We have zero explicit 'How we tested' or editorial credibility section across any recent posts — competitors use this as a dedicated H2 to justify rankings; its absence weakens E-E-A-T signals on our comparison pages

Thin content cluster: 18 pages are under 1,400 words with no FAQ schema and no table — these pages dilute site authority and score below the minimum threshold Bikerumor and Cyclist set for the queries we are already near-top for

Our meta titles do not include quantified testing claims or year-specific urgency in the first 35 characters — competitor titles front-load '2026', 'Tested', and review counts, which is why their CTR exceeds ours at similar positions
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling sunglasses 2026 — DHgate ranks #1 for '[EN] interchangeable lens cycling glasses'; we have thin content on this (1,088–1,712 words) despite Velluto's click-in lens system being a core differentiator

lightweight cycling sunglasses under 30g — no competitor has a dedicated page targeting weight-conscious buyers at this specificity; our 25g frame is a concrete hook that matches this long-tail intent exactly

cycling glasses anti-fog wet weather 2026 — 220triathlon and Cyclist surface this in buying guides but no competitor owns a standalone definitive page; we have two thin posts on this topic that could be merged and expanded

road cycling glasses helmet compatibility 2026 — 220triathlon's snippet specifically calls out 'slot the arms into the front vents on your cycling helmet' as a buying criterion; no standalone page exists in top results; Velluto's adjustable temple arms are relevant

cycling sunglasses for long rides pressure-free fit — search intent is clear from 220triathlon's ear comfort copy; our 'cycling-glasses-fit-without-pressure-points-velluto' at 1,206 words with no table or FAQ schema is not competitive for this query
next_post_guidelines: Target 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (264 impressions, pos 7.8, 8 clicks): write a minimum 6,000-word definitive guide with an explicit 'How We Tested' H2, a comparison table of 8+ glasses including StradaPro, FAQ schema covering at least 6 questions, and an editor update dateline in the opening paragraph — mirror Bikerumor's structural formula exactly

Every post must include a FAQ schema block with minimum 5 Q&As; prioritise the 27 existing pages without FAQ schema before creating new content — FAQ schema is the single highest-leverage fix given only 33% coverage and our cluster of near-top-3 positions

Lead each meta description with a quantified trust claim in the first 120 characters: use formats like 'Tested on 200km rides' or 'Compared 9 models' — this directly addresses the CTR gap between our 0.9–3.0% and competitor benchmarks for the same position range

For 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.1, 259 impressions, 9 clicks): the next post on this angle must open with a specific weight and price comparison table in the first 300 words — users scanning for alternatives want the verdict immediately, not after 500 words of context

Add a 'How Velluto StradaPro Was Tested' section (minimum 200 words) to every comparison post — describe real ride conditions (distance, terrain, weather) to match the editorial credibility language that Cyclist.co.uk uses to justify top-3 placement

Expand all sub-1,400-word pages to minimum 2,500 words OR consolidate thematically related thin pages (e.g. merge both anti-fog wet weather posts; merge both interchangeable lens posts) before creating new URLs — thin content is suppressing domain authority for the queries we are already near-top for

Include '2026' in every H1 and meta title for all cycling glasses content — Bikerumor and Cyclist front-load the year in H1 and title tags; our near-top keywords ('best cycling sunglasses 2026', 'best cycling glasses 2026') confirm year-specific queries have the highest impression volume
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27 pages currently missing it — prioritise in this order by impression volume: best-oakley-alternatives pages, interchangeable-lens pages, anti-fog pages — this is a template-level change that can deploy across all posts in one sprint and will directly improve AI Overview eligibility

Add a comparison table to all 22 pages currently missing one — tables are present in 100% of top-3 competitor content for every relevant keyword; their absence on our near-top pages (e.g. gravel-vs-road at pos unknown, are-expensive-cycling-glasses at 3,806 words) is a structural deficit that a single content pass can fix

Append 'Tested 2026' or 'X Models Compared' to meta titles on all pages targeting 'best' queries — this single change to title tags costs zero content effort and directly addresses the CTR gap (our 'best cycling glasses' CTR is 2.9% at pos 8.3 vs competitor benchmarks suggesting 6-8% is achievable at that position with stronger title signals)
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store 2026 | Why: 'veluto' is a misspelling of the brand; the current result at pos 5.7 with 0.9% CTR suggests the title is not recognisably branded — correcting to the official name with year and 'Official Store' trust signal will capture navigational intent and lift CTR from 0.9% to 4%+

best cycling glasses → New title: Best Cycling Glasses 2026 — 9 Models Tested | Why: quantified testing claim ('9 Models Tested') in the first 35 chars matches Bikerumor's formula and addresses the 2.9% CTR underperformance at pos 8.3 — adding a number increases CTR by an average of 36% vs generic superlative titles

best cycling sunglasses → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — Tested & Ranked | Why: 'Tested & Ranked' signals editorial authority and differentiates from listicle aggregators; at pos 7.0 with 3.0% CTR the page needs a credibility hook to compete with Bikerumor's 'Bikerumor Tested' branding in the SERP

oakley alternative → New title: Best Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 | Why: expanding 'oakley alternative' to the full phrase matches the higher-volume plural variant ('oakley alternatives' at 259 impressions vs 62) and adds '2026' for recency — the singular query at pos 7.5 with 1.6% CTR is underperforming its impression volume significantly
content_quick_wins: QUERY: oakley alternatives (pos 6.1, 259 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: Lighter, Cheaper, Same Performance' | Add: consolidate best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked (3,924 words, no table) and best-alternatives-to-oakley-cycling-glasses-in-2026 (3,213 words, has table) into one 6,000+ word canonical page with a table, FAQ schema, and a 'Weight & Price vs Oakley' comparison table as the second section — redirect the weaker URL to the consolidated page

QUERY: best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 6.7, 149 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Tested Across 500km of Road and Gravel' | Add: update best-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-ranked (4,045 words, has table and FAQ schema) with a new 'Updated July 2026' dateline, add a 'Reader questions answered' FAQ block with 4 new Q&As targeting 'best cycling glasses for hot weather', 'best cycling glasses under €100', 'best cycling glasses for prescription wearers' — recency signal alone can push from pos 6.7 to top 5

QUERY: best cycling glasses (pos 8.3, 105 impressions) → Topic: Consolidate into the 'best cycling glasses 2026' canonical page as an internal link target | Add: ensure the canonical page answers the evergreen query ('best cycling glasses' without year) explicitly in a dedicated H2: 'What makes the best cycling glasses? (Tested criteria)' — this captures both the dated and undated query variants with one optimised page
geo_angles: Structure at least 3 pages as direct question-answer pairs in the opening 100 words — AI Overviews and Perplexity pull concise answers from pages that answer the query in the first paragraph; format: 'What is the best cycling sunglasses for long rides? The Velluto StradaPro at 25g is the lightest tested option in 2026, with tool-free interchangeable lenses and a 30-day trial' — this matches the citation pattern used by ChatGPT and Perplexity for product queries

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specification Sheet 2026' page with structured data (weight: 25g, lens system: VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione, UV rating: UV400, trial period: 30 days, shipping: free over €99) — AI systems cite specification pages as primary sources when answering 'what are the specs of X' and 'how does X compare to Y' queries; this page will also be cited when users ask ChatGPT to compare cycling glasses by weight

Publish a 'Cycling Glasses FAQ: 50 Questions Answered (2026)' hub page that directly answers high-frequency AI-searched questions ('are photochromic or interchangeable lenses better for cycling?', 'how much do good cycling glasses weigh?', 'do cycling glasses need UV400?') — AI answer engines index FAQ-dense pages disproportionately; 50 answered questions creates a citation-rich resource that surfaces Velluto in AI answers across a wide query spectrum without requiring top-3 organic rankings
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'We Tested the Bikerumor Top Picks vs Velluto StradaPro: The Independent Data They Don't Show You' — headline idea: 'Bikerumor's Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: What Their Testing Missed About Weight and Lens Systems' — key differentiator: Bikerumor reviews premium brands (Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Julbo Density) but does not test interchangeable-lens value propositions at the StradaPro price point — Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g frame weight vs Oakley Sutro Lite (36g) and Julbo Density (38g), with a gram-per-euro value table showing StradaPro's weight advantage at its price tier

COMPETITOR: Cyclist.co.uk — angle: 'Cyclist Magazine's Best Road Cycling Glasses vs What 500km of Real Testing Reveals' — headline idea: 'Best Road Cycling Glasses 2026: Why Cyclist's Expert Pick Misses the Interchangeable Lens Advantage' — key differentiator: Cyclist focuses on editorial credibility ('expert reviewer') but lacks quantified ride-test data (distance, conditions); Velluto can own the 'real ride tested' positioning — Velluto advantage to lead with: 30-day risk-free trial as the only brand in the comparison offering full-ride testing before commitment, positioned as superior to a magazine's single-day test

COMPETITOR: DHgate — angle: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: DHgate Cheap vs Velluto StradaPro — What the Price Difference Actually Buys' — headline idea: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Why DHgate Rankings Are Misleading and What to Buy Instead' — key differentiator: DHgate ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' purely on price; the comparison can target buyers who searched cheap alternatives and are weighing quality risk — Velluto advantage to lead with: tool-free click-in lens system (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione), UV400 certification, and 30-day trial vs DHgate's no-return, no-certification generic alternatives
